verify.cooldown: One sec - wait a moment, then tap **Verify** again.
verify.instructions: Type the characters shown below (not case-sensitive). New image each try.
verify.expired: That code expired - tap **Verify** for a fresh one.
verify.roleMissing: Passed - but the verified role is missing. Ping a mod.
verify.tooMany: Too many wrong tries - tap **Verify** for a new image.
verify.wrong: That's not right - tap **Verify** for a new image and try again. ({left} left)
verify.panelText:
**Verify & Agree to the Rules**
Click **Verify** and complete verification. Verifying confirms you’ve read and agree to the rules above, and unlocks the server. This is all done within Discord, you will not be brought to an external site.
verify.pickStyle: How would you like to verify? Pick one:
verify.posInstructions: 🧩 **Find where the piece fits.** Click the number of the slot whose hole matches the piece. Get {total} in a row to verify.
verify.posAgain: ✅ Correct! One more to be sure — round {n} of {total}.
verify.posWrong: ❌ Not that slot. Here is a fresh puzzle — {left} tries left.
verify.testMode: 🧪 **Test mode** — you are staff and already verified, so this run will not change any roles.
verify.testPassed: 🧪 Test passed! A member would receive the verified role now.

appeal.invalid: That appeal link is no longer valid - you may already have been unbanned, or already sent an appeal for this ban.
appeal.busy: The mod team's inbox is busy right now - please try again in a minute.
appeal.sent: ✅ Your appeal was sent to **{guild}**'s mod team. If they approve it, I'll DM you a fresh invite.
appeal.unreachable: Sorry - I couldn't reach that server's mod team ({error}). Try again in a moment.
appeal.dmIntro: ⚠️ You were banned by a **MadHoney** honeypot - a hidden channel that exists only to catch spam bots. Anything posted there is banned automatically.
appeal.dmAsk: If you're a real person who ended up in one by mistake, you can ask **{guild}**'s mod team to take another look:
appeal.approvedInvite: ✅ Your appeal to **{guild}** was approved. Rejoin here: {invite}
appeal.approvedNoInvite: ✅ Your appeal to **{guild}** was approved. Ask a member for a fresh invite to rejoin.
appeal.denied: Your appeal to **{guild}** was reviewed and not approved.

reply.support:
🍯 **MadHoney support**
Questions, bug reports, and feature requests are all welcome — join the support server: {invite}
reply.needManageServer: You need **Manage Server** to use MadHoney controls.
reply.noHoneypotYet: No honeypot channel set yet - run `/madhoney setup` first.
reply.banshareOn: 🌐 **Ban sharing ON** - users on the universal MadHoney ban list (caught by any server's honeypot) are auto-banned when they join here. Run `/madhoney bansync` to also ban everyone already on the list.
reply.banshareOff: 🔒 **Isolated** - this server acts only on its own honeypot catches. (Your catches still feed the universal list for servers that opt in.)
reply.modeArmed: 🍯 **Honeypot armed.** Accounts that post in the honeypot are banned immediately.
reply.modeReview: ⏸ **Honeypot set to hold for review.** Honeypot posts are reported to your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons instead of an instant ban - a mod decides. Not recommended for busy servers, and it needs a log channel set.
reply.modeDisarmed: ⭘ **Honeypot disarmed.** The trap is off - nobody gets banned until you arm it again.
reply.modeReviewNoLog: ⚠️ You have no log channel set, so held posts have nowhere to go. Set one under **Staff & log**.

log.reportBanned: 🍯 **The following message was posted in the honeypot** (<#{channel}>) **and the user has been banned.**
log.reportFailed: 🍯 **The following message was posted in the honeypot** (<#{channel}>) **and the user has been ⚠️ NOT banned (ban failed - check my permissions).**
log.reviewHeld: ⏸ **Held for review.** {tag} (`{id}`) posted in the honeypot (<#{channel}>). **Nobody has been banned yet.**
log.banshareReport: 🌐 **Auto-banned on join** - this user is on the universal MadHoney ban list (caught by another server's honeypot).
log.banSyncSummary: 🌐 **Shared ban list synced** - banned **{n}** account(s) caught by other MadHoney servers.
log.webhookAlert: 🪝 **A webhook posted in the honeypot** (<#{channel}>) and its message was deleted. Webhooks aren't members, so they can't be banned - open **Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks** and delete any you don't recognize.
log.appealForward: 📩 **Ban appeal** - <@{id}> ({tag}, `{id}`) was banned by the honeypot and is asking you to take another look.
log.unbannedBy: ✅ **Unbanned** by {mod} - they can rejoin (send them a fresh invite; ban-share won't re-ban them).
log.permReminder: 🍯 Heads up from **MadHoney** about **{guild}**: I couldn't post to your configured mod-log channel. I need **View Channel** and **Send Messages** there - it's where ban reports and member appeals (with the Approve/Deny buttons) are posted, so the appeal process won't work until I can post to it. Fix my permissions on that channel, or pick a different log channel: {dash}
log.gateCatchup: 🔒 Gated {n} channel(s) that were created while I was offline, to keep the verification gate closed.

setup.title: ## MadHoney setup
setup.dashHint: 💡 **The web dashboard is the easy way to do this.** {dash} is far more powerful and user-friendly than these chat commands - drag-and-drop channel gating, a live banner designer, appeals, and clearer guidance. These commands work too, if you prefer Discord.
setup.verifiedRole: **Verified role:** {role} - pick an existing role, or create one first (Server Settings → Roles, e.g. "Verified").
setup.verifyChannel: **Verify channel:** {channel} - where the Verify button lives. Your **#rules** channel is the classic spot.
setup.honeypotChannel: **Honeypot channel:** {channel} - create a decoy channel bots will post in. Name it like a real channel: `general-2`, `general2`, `chat-2`. Anyone who posts there is banned.
setup.staffRole: **Staff role (optional):** {role} - members with it are never trapped (admins with **Manage Server** and the owner are always exempt). Set it under **Staff & log**.
setup.logChannel: **Log channel (optional):** {channel} - every honeypot ban is reported there with an **Unban** button. Set it under **Staff & log**.
setup.hpReview: ⏸ **Hold for review** - a mod approves each hit
setup.onboarding: ⚠️ If you use Discord **Onboarding**, make sure it does NOT auto-grant the verified role, or the captcha is bypassable.
setup.accessibility: ♿ Honeypots are visual traps - not recommended for servers serving visually impaired communities. At minimum, name the honeypot in your rules so text-to-speech users hear the warning.
setup.whenReady: When all three are set, run **/madhoney deploy**.
setup.clash: ❌ Verify and honeypot must be **different** channels.

more.title: ## Staff & log
more.staffRole: **Staff role:** {role} - anyone with this role can post in the honeypot without being banned. The owner and anyone with **Manage Server** are always exempt; set this for mods who don't have that permission.
more.logChannel: **Log channel:** {channel} - a staff-only channel; every honeypot ban is reported there with an **Unban** button in case someone trips it by accident.

deploy.title: ## Deploy MadHoney
deploy.dashHint: 💡 Prefer buttons and a live preview? Do all of this on the dashboard: {dash}
deploy.step1: 1. **Grandfather members** - give everyone already here the verified role
deploy.step2: 2. **Post Verify panel** - the button + captcha, in your verify channel
deploy.step3: 3. **Post honeypot banner** - the warning image (design it with `/madhoney banner`)

banner.preview: Banner preview - saved. Tweak with `/madhoney banner`, or post it:

stats.title: MadHoney - Statistics
stats.subtitle: Live numbers across every server running MadHoney.
stats.chartAria: Unique spammers caught per day over the last 30 days
stats.chartNote: Each spammer is counted once, on the day they were first caught - a bot banned in several servers is one spammer, not many.
stats.onlyYou: Only shown to you - the servers you own, have Manage Server in, or hold a staff/admin role in.

guild.refreshTitle: Re-fetch roles, channels and members from Discord
guild.armDescArmed: Anyone who posts in the honeypot is banned immediately.
guild.armDescReview: Honeypot posts are held and reported to your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons - a mod decides. Not recommended for busy servers.
guild.armDescDisarmed: The trap is off - nobody gets banned. Arm it once everything is set up.
guild.armReviewNoLog: Set a log channel below - held posts have nowhere to go otherwise.
guild.guideP1: Why MadHoney works this way. A spam bot never posts once - it blasts every channel it can reach and pings @everyone if it can, and every message leaves that channel marked unread. Banning the bot and deleting the spam doesn't clear those unread markers, so you're left with a whole server flagged for spam nobody should have seen. MadHoney catches the bot in one place before it can touch the rest of your server.
guild.guideP2: Bots aren't smart, and the design leans on that: gate every channel behind the verified role and the only place an unverified account can post is the honeypot. Name it like a real channel (general-2) and an indiscriminate bot walks straight in. Its only content is an image - a human reads the warning and backs off, but a bot can't parse a picture, so it posts anyway and trips the trap. The verified role hides the honeypot from anyone who passed the captcha, so no real member ever gets caught. That's why verification is the recommended setup - it's what keeps humans out of the trap.
guild.guideLi1: Verified role first. Create a role (e.g. "Verified") in Server Settings → Roles, then drag the MadHoney role ABOVE it. If MadHoney's role sits below the verified role, gating and grandfathering fail with a permission error - this is the single most common mistake.
guild.guideLi2: Configure below. Pick the verified role, your verify channel (usually #rules), and a honeypot channel. Optionally add a staff role and a log channel.
guild.guideLi3: Grandfather members. Run this first so everyone already in the server gets the verified role and nobody is locked out when you gate.
guild.guideLi4: Post the Verify panel and honeypot banner.
guild.guideLi5: Gate channels. Open the drag board, review what MadHoney detected, move anything it misjudged, then apply. Nothing changes until you hit Apply.
guild.tipNaming: 🍯 Naming the honeypot: name it like a real channel so bots post in it. Good: general-2, chat-2, off-topic-2. Bad: honeypot, do-not-post (some spam tools skip those).
guild.tipMode: 🔘 Honeypot mode: the control at the top has three settings. Armed bans on sight (recommended). Off disables the trap while you set up. Review holds each hit in your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons so a mod decides - not recommended for busy servers (a real spam run floods the log), but it works if you want a human in the loop.
guild.tipOnboarding: ⚠️ Discord Onboarding: if you use it, make sure it does NOT auto-grant the verified role, or the captcha can be skipped.
guild.tipPerm: 🔒 Getting a permission error? It's almost always the MadHoney role sitting below your verified role, or missing Manage Roles / Manage Channels. Re-invite MadHoney with ＋ Add server in the top bar, then drag its role to the top of your staff roles.
guild.verifiedRoleHint: Granted after the captcha. MadHoney's own role must sit ABOVE it.
guild.verifyChannelHint: Where the Verify button lives - your #rules channel is the classic spot.
guild.honeypotChannelHint: The trap. Name it like a real channel (general-2). Posting here = instant ban.
guild.logChannelHint: Staff-only channel - each ban is reported there with an Unban button.
guild.deleteHint: How much of a trapped user's recent message history to wipe when banned. Discord's max is 7 days.
guild.staffRoleHint: Never trapped by the honeypot, and can manage MadHoney here.
guild.adminRoleHint: Dashboard access WITHOUT the honeypot exemption - for helpers.
guild.requireVerifyHint: This is the whole point of MadHoney. It gates your channels behind the verified role so the honeypot is the only place an unverified account can post, and it hides the honeypot from real members so no human ever gets caught. Turn this off and the honeypot still bans, but it's visible to everyone, so a member could wander in and get banned - you'd be relying only on the warning banner.
guild.verifOffWarn: ⚠️ Verification is OFF. The honeypot is not hidden from your members, so a human could post in it and get banned. Only the warning banner protects them. We strongly recommend turning verification back on.
guild.gfIntentWarn: ⏳ Grandfathering runs through our helper bot while MadHoney awaits Discord's approval for the Server Members intent. Invite WorkerBee, drag its role above your verified role in Server Settings › Roles, then run Grandfather below — it joins, grants existing members access, and leaves. (Active members are also granted access automatically as they post.)
guild.captchaHint: Harder = longer code and more OCR-defeating distortion. Raise it if bots start solving your captcha; lower it if real members struggle.
guild.botLanguageHint: The language MadHoney speaks in this server - the verify panel, captcha replies, and appeal DMs. (This dashboard follows your own browser language, which is separate.)
guild.verifyMessageHint: Shown above the Verify button. Leave blank to use the default in your bot language.
guild.creditNote: ℹ️ A small "protected by MadHoney" credit line is shown on your verify panel (not the honeypot banner). Want it gone? MadHoney is free and open - self-host it (SELF_HOSTED=true) and you can remove it.
guild.applyList: Apply the universal ban list to this server
guild.applyListHint: Every honeypot catch across all MadHoney servers feeds one list. ON: users on it are banned when they join here (use "Ban from List" below to apply it retroactively). OFF: this server acts only on its own catches - which it keeps either way.
guild.applyListSelfHost: Note: this is a self-hosted instance, so "the list" means only the servers this bot runs — it is not connected to the hosted MadHoney network's ban list, by design. An empty pool just means your own honeypots haven't caught anyone across your servers yet.
guild.letAppeal: Let banned users appeal by DM
guild.letAppealHint: When a honeypot ban happens, MadHoney DMs the user offering to appeal. If they do, the request lands in your log channel with Approve (unban + fresh invite) / Deny buttons. A user only ever sees servers they were actually banned from that turned this on - never anything else.
guild.cardHoneypotSub: The trap channel, what a ban wipes, and the warning banner pinned inside it.
guild.cardVerificationSub: The captcha gate that keeps the honeypot invisible to real members.
guild.cardModerationSub: Where ban reports land, the shared ban list, and the appeal flow.
guild.cardAccessSub: Who can manage MadHoney here, and the language it speaks.
guild.requireVerifyShort: Hides the honeypot behind the verified role, so bots are the only accounts that can find it.
guild.applyListShort: Auto-bans spammers caught by other MadHoney servers when they join here.
guild.letAppealShort: Banned users can appeal by DM; requests land in your log channel with Approve / Deny buttons.
guild.stepConfigH: Verified role, verify channel, and honeypot channel — in the cards below.
guild.stepPanelsH: The Verify button in your verify channel, and the warning banner in the honeypot.
guild.stepArmH: The trap goes live. Use the bar at the top to switch modes any time.
guild.autoGateHint: When a new channel everyone can post in appears, gate it behind the verified role automatically. Read-only announcement channels stay public.
guild.confirmBanSync: Ban {n} listed accounts — click again to confirm
guild.confirmRestore: Ungate all {n} channels — click again to confirm
guild.confirmGf: Start grandfathering {members} members — click again to confirm
guild.turnOnListFirst: Turn on the universal ban list first.
guild.bannerNote:
Live preview - it re-renders as you tweak. Save, then post it from Actions below.
Make it yours. If every MadHoney honeypot looked identical, bots could learn to recognize and skip it. Change the text, colors, font or logo so your banner is one of a kind. (The file is also posted under a random name each time, for the same reason.)
guild.headline: Headline
guild.mentionHighlightHint: Anything written as #channel or @role gets a pill in this color.
guild.roleColoringHint: "Real role colors" pulls each @role's color from Discord; #channels and colorless roles use the custom color.
guild.logoHint: Direct PNG/JPG URL for your own logo, or type none for no logo.
guild.distortionHint: Garbles the text captcha-style so OCR bots can't read the warning and skip the trap. Higher = harder for machines (and slightly harder for humans). Preview it above before you post.
guild.hideCredit: Hide the "protected by MadHoney" credit line
guild.hideCreditHint: You're self-hosting, so this is your call. The credit shows as a small link on your verify panel (not on the honeypot banner, so the decoy stays generic).
guild.deployHeader: Deploy — run 1 → 4 in order on first setup
guild.step1Desc: Gives the verified role to every human already in the server, so gating never locks anyone out. Bots and already-verified members are skipped.
guild.step2Desc: Posts the Verify button in the verify channel. New members click it, read a captcha, and receive the verified role.
guild.step3Desc: Posts the warning image (designed above) into the honeypot channel. Re-run after changing the banner.
guild.step4Desc: Opens the channel picker: MadHoney classifies every channel (public / private / admin) and you choose exactly which to hide behind the verified role. Nothing changes until you apply.
guild.ungateDesc: Reverses gating: returns the channels MadHoney gated to how they looked before. Admin channels it never touched are left alone.
guild.banSyncDesc: Bans everyone on the universal ban list now, instead of waiting for them to join. Needs the universal list turned ON above.
guild.noBans: No bans logged in this server yet.
guild.captchaStyleHint: Text is the classic OCR-defeating code. Fit-the-piece is a one-click shape match — easier on phones and for members who struggle with distorted text. Member’s choice offers both.
guild.captchaTestHint: Preview updates with the pickers above (save to apply). For a live test: staff who already have the verified role can click Verify on your panel — it runs the real flow without changing roles.
guild.gfEstimate: ⏱️ ≈ up to {mins} min for {members} members — Discord limits role changes to about 1 per second per server, so large servers take a while. It runs in the background and resumes if interrupted.

msg.bannerSaved: Banner saved. Post it to the honeypot with “Post banner”.
msg.channelClash: ❌ Verify and honeypot must be different channels - not saved.
msg.noteArmed: 🍯 Honeypot armed. Anyone who posts in the honeypot channel is now banned.
msg.noteReview: ⏸ Honeypot set to Hold for review. Honeypot posts go to your log channel with Ban / Dismiss buttons instead of an instant ban.
msg.noteReviewNoLog: ⚠️ Set a log channel below so held posts have somewhere to go.
msg.noteDisarmed: ⭘ Honeypot disarmed. The trap is off - nobody gets banned until you arm it again.
msg.jobRunning: A job is already running - watch the bar below.
msg.refreshed: Refreshed roles and channels from Discord.
msg.gateReset: Cleared manual moves - channels are back to auto-detected placement.

gate.title: Gate channels
gate.notConfigBody: Finish {config} first - I need the verified role, verify channel and honeypot channel.
gate.intro: Drag channels between the columns to choose what MadHoney does with each (or tap a channel to cycle it). The colored dot shows what MadHoney auto-detected - if it guessed wrong, just move the channel. Dragging a category moves all its channels with it, like Discord. Your moves are saved, so the next scan remembers them.
gate.zoneGateHint: Hidden behind the verified role. Unverified accounts can't see these.
gate.zonePublicHint: Stays visible to everyone, verified or not.
gate.zoneLeaveHint: MadHoney won't touch these at all.
gate.honeypotInfo: 🍯 Honeypot (open to unverified, hidden from verified):
gate.cantAccess: ⚠️ Can't access {n} channel(s) (hidden from me): {list}. Grant the MadHoney role View there, or temporarily give it Administrator.
gate.gfRunningWarn: ⚠️ Grandfathering is running right now. If you gate channels before it finishes, existing members who don't have the verified role yet will be locked out. Wait for it to complete (see Actions on the server page).
gate.gfNotDoneWarn: ⚠️ You haven't grandfathered existing members yet. Gating now locks out everyone without the verified role until they pass verification. Run Grandfather first (Actions) so current members keep access.

home.title: MadHoney - Your servers
home.subtitle: Pick a server to configure MadHoney, or add it to a new one.
home.getStartedBody: MadHoney isn't in any of your servers yet. Add it to one below, then run through setup here.
home.noServers: No servers where you have Manage Server (or a MadHoney staff/admin role). Ask an admin, or add MadHoney to a server you own.

err.loginBody: The bot owner hasn't set the OAuth client secret. You can still {add} and run /madhoney setup in Discord.
err.e403Body: You don’t manage this server — you need Manage Server there (or that server’s staff / dashboard admin role).
err.e404Body: This page doesn’t exist — consider it a little honeypot. Unlike the bots we catch, you’re free to walk away.
err.e500Body: Something went wrong on our side. It’s been logged — give it a moment and try again.
err.e401Body: You need to log in with Discord before this page will talk to you.

act.gLineReadonly: STAYS PUBLIC ({n} read-only for everyone - announcements etc., no spam risk): {list}
act.gLineHoney: HONEYPOT (open to everyone, hidden from verified): {list}
act.gNoAccess:
⚠️ CAN'T ACCESS ({n}) - I can't see these, so I can't gate them: {list}
   Fix: temporarily give the MadHoney role Administrator (Server Settings → Roles), run gate again, then remove it - or grant the MadHoney role View Channel on each.
act.roleNotFound: Verified role not found - re-run setup.
act.anCant: ⚠️ New channel #{channel} is public but I can't gate it - grant the MadHoney role View Channel + Manage Roles there, or re-run gating from the dashboard.
act.unNothing: Nothing to restore - no channels I gated on record, and none look orphaned.
act.unRecovered: (recovered orphaned channels - no gate record existed)
act.unDone: Restored {ok} channels to their pre-gate visibility{note}{failedPart}
act.errPerm:
{msg}

What this means: Discord blocked the change because of MadHoney's permissions. Fix it in Server Settings → Roles:
  1. Drag the MadHoney role ABOVE your verified role (a bot can't touch roles or channels above its own). This is the #1 cause of this error.
  2. Make sure the MadHoney role still has Manage Roles, Manage Channels and Ban Members. The quickest fix is to re-invite MadHoney with the "Add server" button in the top bar, which re-grants everything.
Then run the action again.
act.errAccess:
{msg}

What this means: MadHoney can't see one or more channels, so it can't change them. Either grant the MadHoney role "View Channel" on those channels, or temporarily give MadHoney the Administrator permission (Server Settings → Roles), run the action, then remove it.
act.pfRoleMissing: Verified role not found - re-run setup and pick one.
act.pfNoManageRoles: I don't have the Manage Roles permission here - re-invite me with the link on the site, or grant it to my role.
act.pfBelow: My role ("{me}") is BELOW the verified role ("{role}") in the role list, so Discord won't let me grant it. Fix: Server Settings → Roles → drag "{me}" above "{role}", then try again.
act.sbOff: Ban sharing is OFF for this server - turn it on first, then sync.
act.sbNoBan: I don't have the Ban Members permission here.
act.sbDone: Ban sync: {added} banned from the shared list, {skipped} already banned here{failedPart}. Pool size: {pool}.

landing.title: MadHoney — Free Discord Anti-Spam Bot (Honeypot + Captcha Verification)
landing.metaDesc: MadHoney is a free honeypot and captcha verification bot for Discord. It gates your channels behind a verified role so spam bots trap themselves and get banned, while humans just click Verify. Open dashboard, one-command setup.
landing.heroSub: MadHoney doesn't try to recognize spam. It wires up your server so only a bot would ever trigger the trap. Humans click Verify, read a captcha, and get in. Bots post in the decoy channel and get banned, messages wiped, before anyone wakes up.
landing.whyLead: A spam bot never posts just once. It blasts every channel it can reach and tries to ping @everyone on the way through. Every message lights up that channel with an unread marker, and here's the part that stings: ban the bot and delete the spam, and those unread dots usually stay. You come back to a whole server flagged with "new messages" for spam nobody should have seen. MadHoney's job is to stop that before it starts - to catch the bot in one place, before it can touch the rest of your server.
landing.step1P: Every channel is hidden behind a verified role. A new account sees exactly two things: your rules channel… and one decoy.
landing.step2P: Humans click Verify, read an image captcha, type the code, and the whole server unlocks. Takes about ten seconds. No DMs, no third-party site.
landing.step3P: The decoy is named like a real channel (#general-2) and it's the only place an unverified account can post. Spam bots post everywhere they can, so they post there first. Instant ban, plus their last 7 days of messages get deleted. Verified humans never even see it.
landing.thinkH2: Bots aren't smart. That's the whole plan.
landing.f1P: A spam bot doesn't pick targets. It dumps its message in every channel it can see and pings @everyone if it can. Gate everything behind the verified role and the only door left open is the honeypot.
landing.f2P: Name the decoy like a normal channel (general-2, chat-2) and an indiscriminate bot walks straight in. It looks like just another place to spam.
landing.f3P: The honeypot's only content is an image. A human reads "don't post here" and backs off; a bot can't parse a picture, so it posts anyway and trips the trap.
landing.f4P: The verified role hides the honeypot from everyone who passed the captcha. Real members never even see the channel, so the only thing that ever lands in it is a bot.
landing.showP: MadHoney generates the hazard banner that sits pinned in the honeypot: your headline, body text, colors, fonts, even your community's logo. Design it with /madhoney banner or in the dashboard. The one you're looking at is rendered live by the bot.
landing.feat1P: /madhoney setup walks you through role and channel pickers, then /madhoney deploy grandfathers existing members, posts the panels, and gates your channels. There's a dry-run before anything changes.
landing.feat2P: Every ban is reported to a staff channel: who, when, and what they posted. One-click Unban button if a curious human tripped the wire.
landing.feat3P: Every honeypot catch, across every server, lands on one list. Opt in and known spammers are banned the moment they join yours (or all at once with Ban from List). Opt out anytime - your own catches stay yours. An Undo removes a user from the list everywhere.
landing.feat4P: The trap fires on where a message was posted, never on what it says. No external database, no analytics. Staff and owners are always exempt.
landing.feat5P: Log in with Discord and manage any server where you have Manage Server: config, verify message, banner designer, deploy actions, and the ban log.
landing.feat6P: Read it, fork it, self-host it. One Node process, three files of state. github.com/nomadsgalaxy/MadHoney · OCL v1.1 + SWAtt.
landing.startLi1: Invite MadHoney and grant the requested permissions.
landing.startLi2: Create a Verified role (MadHoney's role must sit above it) and a decoy channel like #general-2.
landing.startLi3: Run /madhoney setup and pick the role, your rules channel, the decoy, and an optional staff log channel.
landing.startLi4: Run /madhoney deploy and click through: grandfather → post panels → gate (dry-run, then apply).
landing.startDash: or set it up from the dashboard
landing.warnOnboard: Using Discord Onboarding? Make sure it does NOT auto-grant the verified role, otherwise the captcha is a decoration.
landing.warnA11y: A note on accessibility. A honeypot is a visual trap, and members who rely on text-to-speech or a screen reader can't always tell a decoy from a real channel. We don't recommend honeypot anti-spam for servers that cater to visually impaired communities. If you run one anyway, it's imperative that your rules state plainly that a honeypot channel exists and which channel it is, so TTS users hear the warning too. A human who trips it can always be unbanned from the log channel, but don't let your most vulnerable members be the ones who find the tripwire.
landing.donateH2: Hosted for free. Always will be.
landing.donateLead: MadHoney is free to add and free to run. No paywalls, no premium tier, nothing locked behind an upgrade. Running the bot and dashboard does cost real money for servers and hosting, though. If MadHoney keeps your community clean, chipping in helps cover those costs and keeps it free for everyone.
landing.footTagline:
Honeypot + captcha verification for Discord.
Made with 🧡 by Nomads Galaxy.
landing.costTitle: What it actually costs to run
landing.costPower: Bare-metal electricity (MadHoney’s share, at residential rates)
landing.costFailover: Cloud failover standby (kicks in if the bare-metal server goes down)
landing.costCloudflare: Cloudflare edge — Workers & D1 database (free at this scale; cost grows with the database)
landing.costNote: Computed live from this instance’s real configuration — {watts} W of server share at {rate}/kWh. Hosting is donated by the maintainer; Ko-fi support goes directly toward these costs.
landing.costStory: I wanted to tinker with a dynamic cost estimator to help me figure out how much my projects cost me. I thought this would be a neat data point to track and show, for full transparency. If you wish to help offset this cost, feel free to donate via Ko-fi. MadHoney will always be free.
